shared_features: Momentum: streak (consecutive count going backwards from current position)
Frequency: count in sliding window of last 10 periods
Both measure RECENT ACTIVITY of each zodiac

root_cause.explanation: Both signals measure 'recent appearances' but in different ways. When a zodiac appears consecutively (streak >= 2), its frequency count in the sliding window also increases. This creates high prediction overlap even though the features are distinct.
root_cause.evidence: When Momentum streak >= 2, Frequency count >= 2 in 7/7 cases (100%)

key_observation: error_corr is very low (0.0238) meaning these experts make DIFFERENT errors - this is good for ensemble diversity. The issue is not error correlation but prediction overlap.
recommended_fix: Do NOT modify Momentum or Frequency formulas. The structural overlap is inherent. Instead, adjust the diversity penalty threshold in ExpertDiversityPenalty or accept this pair as a known structural correlation. The ensemble already applies Jaccard penalty at threshold 0.8 for similar expert pairs.
